author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
praveśiyā antaḥpure, nārīgaṇe śānta kare,
śrīvāsa amiya⋅upadeśe
śuna pāgalinī-gaṇa, śoka kara akāraṇa,
kibā duḥkha thāke kṛṣṇāveśe

kṛṣṇa nitya suta jā’ra, śoka kabhu nāhi tā’ra,
anitye āsakti sarvanāśa
āsiyācha e saṁsāre, kṛṣṇa bhajibāra tare,
nitya⋅tattve karaha vilāsa

e dehe jāvat sthiti, kara kṛṣṇa-candre rati,
kṛṣṇe jāna, dhana⋅jana⋅prāṇa
e deha⋅anuga jata, bhāi⋅bandhu⋅pati⋅suta,
anitya sambandha bali’ māna

kebā kā’ra pati⋅suta, anitya⋅sambandha⋅kṛta,
cāhile rākhite nāre tā’re
karama⋅vipāka⋅phale, suta ha’ye baise kole,
karmakṣaye āra raite nāre

ite sukha⋅duḥkha māni’, adhogati labhe prāṇī,
kṛṣṇapada haite paḍe dūre
śoka sambariyā ebe, nāmānande maja’ sabe,
bhakativinoda⋅vāñchā pūre
en_translation:
Entering the inner chambers, Śrīvāsa pacified the women with nectarean instructions: "Listen, O mad women, you lament without cause; what sorrow can remain when one is absorbed in Kṛṣṇa?

One whose eternal son is Kṛṣṇa never has sorrow; attachment to the temporary is utter ruin. You have come to this world for the purpose of worshiping Kṛṣṇa; therefore, delight in the eternal truth.

As long as there is existence in this body, develop attachment for Kṛṣṇa⋅candra; know Kṛṣṇa as your wealth, people, and life. Consider all those connected to this body—brothers, friends, husband, son—as temporary relationships.

Who is whose husband or son? Even if you wish, you cannot keep these temporary relationships. As a result of past actions, a son sits on your lap, but when the effect of karma is finished, he cannot remain.

Considering these temporary things as happiness and sorrow, the living entity attains a lower destination, falling far from Kṛṣṇa's feet. Therefore, suppressing your grief now, everyone immerse yourselves in the bliss of the Holy Name; thus fulfill the desire of Bhaktivinoda."
